position: First token of the request line (command label)
explanation: The FTP command label identifies the request operation; servers use it to determine the command semantics and which reply codes and actions to return. Different commands produce command-specific replies and behaviors (e.g., RETR triggers data-transfer replies, USER triggers authentication replies).

position: Argument of the TYPE command (first parameter)
explanation: Specifies the data representation type for subsequent transfers; server replies (e.g., 200) and transfer behavior (and responses to SIZE/REST) differ depending on the TYPE selected.

position: Optional secondary parameter of the TYPE command (format/format-code)
explanation: When present with TYPE=A or TYPE=E, the format-code selects the ASCII/EBCDIC format effectors; servers acknowledge and apply different format behavior (affecting SIZE and data conversion).
Specifies file structure (how file is to be interpreted); servers record and respond according to structure (e.g., different handling on data operations and reply semantics).
Specifies transfer mode (stream, block, compressed); server behavior and the interpretation of subsequent REST markers and data transfer semantics vary by mode.

Specifies the data-channel protection level; servers accept or reject the requested protection and return command-specific replies (e.g., 200 on acceptance, 504/536/534 on unsupported/unwilling), affecting subsequent data-channel behavior.

position: Second field inside the EPRT command (address-family number)
explanation: Identifies the network protocol/address family to be used for the data connection; the server uses it to select protocol handling and will accept or reject (522) based on supported families.

position: Optional argument of EPSV (network protocol number or the literal ALL)
explanation: When supplied, EPSV requests a specific network protocol or 'ALL' to change allowed data-connection commands; servers choose behavior or reject (522) depending on the requested protocol or the ALL token.

position: Second token (command-name) of the OPTS request
explanation: Names the target FTP command to which the options apply; the server recognizes the command-name and responds (200 or error) indicating whether options for that target command are accepted. (Values are any FTP command that supports options; not enumerated here in the RFC.)

Names the authentication/security mechanism to be used; server replies indicate support, refusal, or requirement for additional security data (e.g., 234/334/504/534). The RFC requires registration of mechanism names but does not enumerate a fixed set in the document.
